summary: 2026-06-26 07:33:17 PDT — Found one IMPORTANT issue against AC6: the existing /api/rates config-check behavior changes. I could not complete npm test/build verification because the read-only sandbox blocked Vitest temp writes and server listen/tmp writes with EPERM.

findings.title: Rate routes now mask missing RATE_STORE_KEY behind auth
findings.claim: AC6 says existing GET/PUT /api/rates behavior is unchanged and that these routes return 503 when RATE_STORE_KEY is unset. In main, requirePresenter checked rateStoreConfigured() before resolving the app context; the new route runs requireIdentity first. With RATE_STORE_KEY unset and no/bad app context, /api/rates now returns 401 before reaching requireRateStore, so the prior 503 readiness/config signal is lost unless the caller has a valid context.
findings.suggestion: Preserve the old config-first gate for GET/PUT /api/rates, for example with a route-specific wrapper that checks rateStoreConfigured() before requireIdentity, while leaving DELETE /api/me/data identity-only. Add a regression test for /api/rates with RATE_STORE_KEY unset and no context.
